anchor_businesses.name: The Archive of Keth
anchor_businesses.type: Scholarly institution — oldest continuously occupied structure in the city
anchor_businesses.age: 3,000 years — the archive predates the current city structure
anchor_businesses.description: Three storeys above ground, two below. The above-ground structure has been rebuilt twice — fire in Year 10,400, structural failure in Year 11,800. The below-ground structure has not been modified. The third sublevel is the oldest human-made interior space in continuous use on Arneth. The smell changes on the stairs down — older, deeper, a quality that is not quite damp but is not quite dry.

lamp_oil.current_level: High — Archive burns significant oil for evening research

food_for_residents.current_level: Adequate — resident scholars are fed from a communal kitchen

water.source: Aquifer access — Archive has its own pump, one of four in the city

writing_materials.current_level: High — the Archive produces its own ink and has significant parchment stock

services: Research access — application required, literacy required, competence assessed
Research patronage — wealthy individuals can fund specific projects

the_third_sublevel.access: First Reader and designated senior archivists only. Technically.
the_third_sublevel.actually: The First Reader has been going down alone before dawn. The night keeper has heard footsteps that do not match the First Reader's schedule. The Pale Company Warden has been conducting survey work there invisibly.
the_third_sublevel.the_unconscious_effect: Strongest in the city at this location. A player who spends time in the third sublevel will experience it at a level that is impossible to dismiss.

hooks: The inscription translation project — accessible through research application
Sera Dawnmere's patron study — a point of Solvanic intelligence contact
The Archivist's eleven-year embedding — discoverable through careful observation
The third sublevel footstep pattern — the night keeper's information

anchor_businesses.name: The Dust Council Chambers
anchor_businesses.description: Deliberately grander than the Archive — newer stone, higher ceilings, the statement of commercial power over scholarly authority. The Council meets in the main chamber three times per ten-day week. The real business happens in the offices surrounding it.
anchor_businesses.access: Public gallery for Council sessions — open to anyone. Office access through appropriate house connection or commercial licensing.
services: The commercial oversight office — where complaints about market manipulation go
hooks: House Solven succession dispute — accessible through either claimant
House Keth-Marren investigation — commercial oversight office
The grain price coordination — Vaela Keth's campaign

anchor_businesses.name: Halverness Factors — Keth Office
anchor_businesses.description: The largest trading house office in Keth's Foreign Quarter. Three storeys, street-level commercial space, two floors of offices. Well-staffed. Maintains correspondence with Halverness offices in the Amber Coast, Thornsveld Marches, and twelve other locations.

resources.correspondence_archive: Three years of records. The most complete commercial intelligence on Keth's import/export flows held by any private entity.
resources.financial_instruments: The mechanism used in both the Keth-Marren manipulation and the consortium buyout is documented here — in terms that implicate the Factors less than they should.

anchor_businesses.name: The Salvage Authority — Keth Office
anchor_businesses.description: Functional, adequately staffed, currently under the specific stress of a Director who cannot get answers about his own jurisdiction.
services: Excavation licensing — required for any plateau ruin work

resources.patrol_routes: The plateau patrol data that includes the Drift Lord movement documentation
resources.site_records: Every licensed excavation on the plateau for 200 years

hooks: Road-guard patrol contracts — immediate work for appropriate characters

anchor_businesses.name: The Eastern Threshold
anchor_businesses.type: Inn and information hub — Dusthalt waystation
anchor_businesses.description: The easternmost point of reliable safety on the road to the deep plateau. Twenty years old as a permanent establishment. The building is solid, well-maintained, built to withstand plateau weather. The common room is the single most information-rich space outside Keth's walls.

water.source: Dusthalt's secondary aquifer — smaller than Keth's but reliable

food.source: Caravan resupply from Keth plus Bren's own preserved provisions
food.notes: Six staged caravans currently at Dusthalt — food consumption running higher than normal

resources.accommodation: Twelve private rooms, common floor space for caravan workers

hooks: Bren Dalt's movement tracking — the unusual parties he has logged
The staged caravans — work, information, the manifest inaccuracy
The last point of reliable safety before the deep plateau

keth_city_guaranteed: Archive of Keth (anchor)
Dust Council Chambers (anchor)
Halverness Factors office (anchor)
Salvage Authority offices (anchor)
Three major inns — one Archive Quarter (scholarly clientele), one Merchant Row (commercial), one Foreign Quarter (international)
The Low Cistern — Lower City inn, no-questions lodging
Main market — permanent stalls, 400 standard capacity
Water Authority office — manages aquifer access fees
Salvage goods market — Varathi material, processed salvage, specialist buyers
Skyfall iron broker — rare materials specialist, one in Keth
Three smithies — general, specialist (salvage metalwork), weapons
Apothecary — Lower City (Morra Duskwalker)
Apothecary — Market Quarter (higher prices, more exotic materials)
Cartographer's office — plateau maps, updated regularly
Letter writer and document services
Money changing — three independent operators
Halverness Factors competitors — two smaller trading houses
Textile import stalls — x4
Food import merchants — x6 (grain, dried goods, preserved meat)
Spice merchants — x3
Lamp oil supplier — critical city resource
Book and manuscript dealer — secondary market for Archive reproductions

keth_city_roll_1d6.4: Wine merchant — high quality, Foreign Quarter
keth_city_roll_1d6.6: Translator for hire — multiple languages, Foreign Quarter

dusthalt_roll_1d4.1: Secondary information broker — competes with Bren Dalt on smaller scale
dusthalt_roll_1d4.2: Salvage goods dealer — buying from outpost workers

keth_water_monopoly.rule: All water in Keth is finite and tracked. The aquifer has a measurable daily output capacity. City population draws approximately 85% of daily output in normal conditions. The third cistern crack reduces buffer capacity.
keth_water_monopoly.player_impact: Water costs iron chits. Bathing, laundry, cooking water all cost. A player spending extended time in Keth will spend on water. A player who discovers the third cistern situation has information the Dust Council wants managed.

keth_food_dependency.rule: Keth produces almost no food. Everything edible comes in by caravan. A bad Solvane harvest season triggers crisis within 60 days.
keth_food_dependency.current_status: Adequate — last Solvane harvest was normal. House Caldren's grain price coordination is causing artificial price pressure, not actual scarcity.
keth_food_dependency.player_impact: Food costs more in Keth than anywhere else in the starting regions. A player with cooking skills and access to market ingredients can reduce this cost significantly.

skyfall_iron.rule: All skyfall iron in Keth must be licensed through the Salvage Authority. Unlicensed possession is illegal — the Authority takes 40% of assessed value as licensing fee retroactively, which most salvagers consider prohibitive.
skyfall_iron.the_grey_market: The Lower City smithy that the Blacksmith's Apprentice spawn hook references is working with unlicensed material. This is knowable and has specific consequences if reported or exploited.
